question.text: Exchange-traded derivatives are treated as non-speculative because:

answer.text: They are traded on recognized stock exchanges, making them eligible for non-speculative treatment even without physical delivery
All derivatives are non-speculative by default
Only options are non-speculative, not futures

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Section 43(5) of the Income Tax Act treats exchange-traded derivatives as non-speculative business even though delivery doesn't occur. This is specific to recognized exchanges. **The Trap:** OTC derivatives without delivery may still be treated as speculative. The exchange-traded status is key. **Cross-Exam Context:** Non-speculative classification is tested in Series I. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Non-speculative losses have broader set-off opportunities than speculative losses.
